title: Nikos Tsapatsoulis
text:
Professor at the Department of Public Communication, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Image Analysis and Synthesis from the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering, National Technical University of Athens (2000). His research has been recognised by the international research community through more than 4000 independent citations.
Research interests: Multimedia Information Retrieval, Web Data Mining, Recommender Systems, Collective Intelligence, Affective Computing.
Email: nicolas.tsapatsoulis@cut.ac.cy

title: Nelly Sergidou
text:
PhD student at the Cyprus University of Technology under the supervision of Associate Professor Vasiliki Triga. Her research focuses on news media and social media agenda in the context of referendum campaigns. Has been involved in European and Horizon projects investigating how European societies deal with their troubled pasts through the analysis of conflict discourses and their impact on European integration.
Research interests: News Media and Social Media, Referendum Campaigns, Electoral Campaigns.

title: Kostas Gemenis
text:
Assistant Professor of Politics and the Internet in the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology. Ph.D. in Politics and International Relations from Keele University, UK. Published in Political Communication, Communication Methods and Measures, and Electoral Studies. Co-designed a module to study conspiracy theories related to the COVID-19 pandemic for the 10th Round of the European Social Survey (ESS).
Research interests: Voting Advice Applications, Political Communication, Quantitative Text Analysis, Social Media and Conspiracy Beliefs.

title: Antonis Charalampous
text:
PhD student at the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology, under the supervision of Associate Professor Constantinos Djouvas. His research focuses on developing new techniques and systems for analysing online discourse.
Research interests: Online Discourse Analysis, Argumentation, Social Media.
Email: ag.charalampous@cut.ac.cy

title: Research
text:
Research at DigiPols
  
  DigiPols research is organised around four clusters that bring together social scientists and computer scientists working on complementary areas. Each is coordinated through the DigiPols Forum.

title: Research
text:
Applied Political Communication
Political communication is concerned with how information is exchanged between political actors — from politicians and governments to civil society and citizens — and the role played by intermediary actors, such as the news media, in this transmission.
The communication linkages are multi-faceted and increasingly digitalised. This cluster is focused on the applied aspects of political communication that involve experimentation, such as lab and field experiments. Most of the ongoing research is associated with the pioneering work of DigiPols researchers on Voting Advice Applications.

14 publications · 5 active projects · Lead: V. Triga

title: Research
text:
Media & Social Media Monitoring
This area is centred on the development of data-collection platforms that track particular domains of interest. Work has been conducted on building real-time data collection during election campaigns, evaluating the communication of policy by the EU, and monitoring multilingual communication practices.

11 publications · 4 active projects · Lead: N. Tsapatsoulis

title: Research
text:
Public Opinion & Argument Mining
This cluster develops techniques and tools for the analysis of public opinion. It includes both traditional forms of public-opinion research, such as surveys, and more recent big-data approaches to opinion mining. A complementary area is argument mining, which draws inspiration from computational linguistics.

9 publications · 3 active projects · Lead: C. Djouvas

title: Research
text:
The DigiPols Forum
The Forum is an umbrella organisation that periodically brings together DigiPols researchers. It keeps researchers abreast of the state-of-the-art and serves as a venue for the exchange of ideas. Crucially, the Forum provides strategic input and guidance to DigiPols’ more specialised research clusters, and is responsible for organising lab events.

Convenes 4×/yr · Open to all members

title: Studying how politics moves through digital signal.
text:
The Digital Politics & Political Communication Research Lab
  Studying how politics moves through digital signal.
  
    DigiPols is an inter-departmental research lab at the Cyprus University of Technology investigating the increasingly digitalised links between politicians, the media, civil society and citizens — through experimentation, Voting Advice Applications and bespoke data platforms.
  
  
    Read the research →
    Meet the lab
  
  
    fig 01 · monitoring panels, live
    4 clusters · est. 2018
  


↳ Research clusters
Four clusters, one forum.


  
    cluster.01
    Applied Political Communication
    Experimental work on how political information moves between political actors, media and citizens — including pioneering research on Voting Advice Applications.
    explore →
  
  
    cluster.02
    Media & Social Media Monitoring
    Bespoke data-collection platforms that track political communication across news media and social media in real time, during election campaigns and beyond.
    explore →
  
  
    cluster.03
    Public Opinion & Argument Mining
    Techniques and tools for the analysis of public opinion, combining surveys with computational methods and argument mining from computational linguistics.
    explore →
  
  
    cluster.04
    The DigiPols Forum
    Umbrella organisation that convenes DigiPols researchers to exchange ideas, share state-of-the-art practice and set strategic direction for the lab.
    explore →

title: About
text:
About the lab
  
  DigiPols pursues an interdisciplinary approach that brings together social scientists and computer scientists conducting complementary research on the intersection of digital technologies and politics.

title: About
text:
Mission
DigiPols provides a research environment for pursuing cutting-edge, theory-driven empirical research for advancing the study of how digital technologies are impacting our institutions of governance — and how politics shapes the development of these technologies.
The lab’s researchers are brought together in the DigiPols Forum, which meets regularly to exchange ideas, discuss best practices and offer strategic input to the four research clusters.

title: About
text:
Host institution
DigiPols is hosted by the Department of Communication and Internet Studies and the Department of Public Communication at the Cyprus University of Technology, Limassol, Cyprus.

title: About
text:
Contact

Email: digipols@cut.ac.cy
Address: Cyprus University of Technology, 30 Archbishop Kyprianos Str., 3036 Limassol, Cyprus
GitHub: github.com/digipols

title: About
text:
Funding
Work at DigiPols has been supported by Horizon Europe, the European Parliament, INTERREG, the Cyprus Research and Innovation Foundation and the Recovery and Resilience Plan of the Republic of Cyprus.

title: Constantinos Djouvas
text:
Associate Professor at the Department of Communication and Internet Studies, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Computer Networks from the City University of New York. Has participated in numerous EU-funded research projects and published in various journals related to Computer Science and Social and Political Science.
Research interests: Big Data, Argument Mining, Social Media Analysis.
Email: costas.tziouvas@cut.ac.cy

title: Nikandros Ioannides
text:
PhD student at Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona under the supervision of Professor Toni Rodon. Holds a master’s degree in Democracy and Comparative Politics from University College London (UCL) and a bachelor’s degree in Communication and Internet Studies from the Cyprus University of Technology (CUT).
Research interests: Contemporary Democracies, Party Politics, Political Participation, Voter-Party Congruence, Public Opinion.

title: Vasiliki Triga
text:
Associate Professor at the Department of Public Communication, Cyprus University of Technology (CUT). Ph.D. in Social and Political Sciences from the European University Institute, Florence. Published in IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics, Southern European Society and Politics, Journal of Common Market Studies, and Public Management. Co-author of Referendums and the European Union: A Comparative Inquiry (Cambridge University Press, 2014).
Research interests: Political Communication, Media and Social Media, Political Parties, Electoral Campaigns.
Email: vasiliki.triga@cut.ac.cy
